Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed declarative clause (noun + possessive + verb + intensifier)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A situation has gone beyond control or repair
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used historically during clan dispute settlements in Du District when elders warned that matters had escalated
First Example Sentence (Cham): Ngwa gwom nyi lo gwan, da ma su la.
Second Example Sentence (Cham): Gwom nyi lo gwan la kot, ngas be ta lo.
Third Example Sentence (Cham): A be yi sha gwom nyi lo gwan.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with object shift (food + verb + locative + noun)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To benefit secretly from a situation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to farming seasons when some workers secretly harvested extra grain

Second Example Sentence (Cham): Ngwa nyam lo da gwom sha, a ta be nyin.
